info.title: csmacnz.Coveralls - a coveralls.io coverage publisher for .Net

options.name: --help
options.description: Show this screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version.

options.name: --input
options.description: The coverage source file location. When --aggregate is used, this is a collection of inputs to publish together. The format is "monocov=coverage.cov;chutzpah=chutzpah.json"

options.name: --output
options.description: The coverage results json will be written to this file if provided.

options.name: --dryrun
options.description: This flag will stop coverage results being posted to coveralls.io

options.name: --useRelativePaths
options.description: This flag, when provided, will attempt to strip the current working directory from the beginning of the source file path.

options.name: --basePath
options.description: When useRelativePaths and a basePath is provided, this path is used instead of the current working directory.

options.name: --opencover
options.description: Reads input as OpenCover data.

options.name: --dynamiccodecoverage
options.description: Reads input as the CodeCoverage.exe xml format.

options.name: --exportcodecoverage
options.description: Reads input as the Visual Studio Coverage Export xml format

options.name: --monocov
options.description: Reads input as monocov results folder.

options.name: --chutzpah
options.description: Reads input as chutzpah json data.

options.name: --lcov
options.description: Reads input as lcov format.

options.name: --ncover
options.description: Reads input as NCover format.

options.name: --reportgenerator
options.description: Reads input as ReportGenerator folder.

options.name: --multiple
options.description: Provide multiple types and files. This mode requires -i to provide values in the format "chutzpah=chutzpahFile.json;opencover=opencoverFile.xml"

options.name: --parallel
options.description: If using the parallel builds. If sent, it will wait for the webhook before completing the build.

options.name: --completeParallelWork
options.description: Submit to Coveralls.io to notify a parallel set of results has finished.

options.name: --repoToken
options.description: The coveralls.io repository token.

options.name: --repoTokenVariable
options.description: The Environment Variable name where the coveralls.io repository token is available. [default: COVERALLS_REPO_TOKEN]

options.name: --commitId
options.description: The git commit hash for the coverage report.

options.name: --commitBranch
options.description: The git branch for the coverage report.

options.name: --commitAuthor
options.description: The git commit author for the coverage report.

options.name: --commitEmail
options.description: The git commit author email for the coverage report.

options.name: --commitMessage
options.description: The git commit message for the coverage report.

options.name: --jobId
options.description: The job Id to provide to coveralls.io.

options.name: --serviceName
options.description: The service-name for the coverage report.

options.name: --serviceNumber
options.description: The service-number for the coverage report.

options.name: --pullRequest
options.description: The github pull request id. Used for updating status on github PRs.

options.name: --treatUploadErrorsAsWarnings
options.description: Exit successfully if an upload error is encountered and this flag is set.

options.name: --serverUrl
options.description:
The coveralls server url [default: https://coveralls.io]
Commit Options:
If --commitId and --commitBranch are provided, all git settings will come from the command line arguments.
If you do not provide any commit information, the correct information will be resolved for you using the following logic:
* If you are on AppVeyor build server ($env:APPVEYOR -eq $true) We will locate the data from the Environment Variables.
* If git is available in the current working directory, the settings will be loaded from git.
What it's for:
Reads your .Net code coverage output data and submits it to
coveralls.io's service. This can be used by your build scripts
or with a CI builder server.
